description: Cross-referenced BB defense ranges for 9-max (full ring) cash games (100bb, standard rake). Key difference from 6-max: early position openers (UTG/UTG+1/UTG+2) have tighter ranges (~10-15%), requiring much tighter BB defense. BTN defense remains wide as BTN has similar steal incentives.

sources.name: PokerCoaching.com (Jonathan Little)
sections: Facing RFI: Big Blind — all 9 position matchups: BB vs UTG/UTG+1, UTG+2, LJ, HJ, CO, BTN, SB
sources.data_quality: PDF DOWNLOADED — all 9-max BB defense scenarios confirmed as distinct charts. Image-based hand grids. Includes both IP and OOP scenarios.
sources.confirmed_structure: 7 distinct BB defense scenarios in the PDF: vs UTG/UTG+1, vs UTG+2, vs LJ, vs HJ, vs CO, vs BTN, vs SB

sources.name: PokerCoaching.com — GTO MTT ranges (text-extracted)
sections: GTO opening ranges by position — 75bb MTT stacks
sources.data_quality: TEXT EXTRACTED — full opening range lists by position in 9-handed setup. Useful for calibrating defense (BB defends against these opens): UTG 13.7%, UTG+1 16.3%, UTG+2 19.2%, LJ 23.5%, HJ 27.9%, CO 37.3%, BTN 54.8%
sources.note: These are 75bb MTT ranges but directionally match 100bb cash game ranges for position-relative widths

sources.name: GTO Wizard Blog
sections: UTG opening range = 17.6% in NL500 100bb 6-max cash (direct link to solution provided)
sources.data_quality: TEXT EXTRACTED — 9-max UTG would be tighter than 6-max UTG. 6-max UTG = ~17.6%; 9-max UTG ≈ 13-15%.

crossReference.agreementLevel: all 3 sources agree on structural principles; PokerCoaching provides full scenario coverage; GTO Wizard and PokerCoaching agree on UTG range widths
crossReference.notes: The key difference from 6-max: 9-max UTG is much tighter. BB defense vs UTG in 9-max is extremely tight — mostly small pocket pairs, strong broadways, and suited connectors. All sources confirm defense broadens significantly vs BTN/CO in both formats.

discrepancy_analysis.UTG_range_width: GTO Wizard (cash 100bb 6-max): UTG = 17.6%. PokerCoaching (MTT 75bb 9-max): UTG = 13.7%. These are different game formats — not contradictory. 9-max cash UTG ~13-15% is reasonable consensus.

assumptions: 100bb effective stacks, 9-max (full ring) cash game, 2.5bb open raise standard, no ante, standard rake.

pokercoaching_opening_ranges_9max_75bb_r.note: Text-extracted from PokerCoaching.com. MTT 75bb ranges but useful for relative position comparison.

scenarios.label: BB vs UTG open 9max
scenarios.rationale: UTG range in 9-max ≈ 13.7% (PokerCoaching text-extracted). Very strong range — mostly big pairs, broadway hands, top aces. BB should defend tight but can still call with hands that dominate the weaker portion of UTG range.

fold: most speculative hands, offsuit aces below AJo, most suited connectors below 87s, most suited kings below KJs
actions.notes: Tightest BB defense. UTG only opens ~13.7%. Only defend hands with strong equity vs this range or good set-mine value.

scenarios.label: BB vs UTG+1 open 9max
scenarios.rationale: UTG+1 range ≈ 16.3% (PokerCoaching text-extracted). Slightly wider than UTG. Add some suited connectors and more pocket pairs.

actions.source: PokerCoaching PDF confirms 'UTG+2 vs UTG/UTG+1' is a combined scenario — defense adapts to merged range

scenarios.label: BB vs CO open 9max
scenarios.rationale: CO range ≈ 37.3% (PokerCoaching text-extracted, 75bb — cash game CO similar). Contains many weaker hands. BB defends wider with calls and some 3-bets.

scenarios.label: BB vs BTN open 9max
scenarios.rationale: BTN range ≈ 54.8% (PokerCoaching text-extracted). Wide range of weak hands. BB defends very wide. Similar to 6-max but slightly tighter as 9-max BTN opens slightly stronger hands on average.

fold: weak offsuit junk — K7o and worse, Q7o and worse, J7o and worse
actions.notes: Wide defense similar to 6-max. PokerCoaching confirms 'BB vs BTN' as separate chart scenario.
